text.title: map-reduce-send-fanout Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 high-impact failure modes for map-reduce-send-fanout with detectors and fixes that map back to rules in 01-core-rules.xml.

antipattern.symptom: Rate-limit cliff: 60 concurrent calls → all fail
antipattern.fix: Wrap with asyncio.Semaphore(8-20); same pattern for Send().
antipattern.detector: Provoke with 100-item list; observe in-flight count peaks.

antipattern.symptom: Super-step fails atomically; non-idempotent branches double-billed on retry
antipattern.root-cause: Branch had a write side-effect; not idempotent.
antipattern.fix: Tag branches as @idempotent; if not, route to a queue+workers instead.
antipattern.detector: Inject a forced fail mid-fan-out; audit ledger for double writes.

antipattern.root-cause: Default reducer overwrites instead of accumulating.
antipattern.fix: Use Annotated[list, operator.add] or explicit merge; never plain dict update.
antipattern.detector: Run with 10 items; result length must equal 10.

antipattern.symptom: Hidden shared state: every branch writes to the same logger handler
antipattern.root-cause: Logger handler is module-global; concurrent writes interleave.
antipattern.fix: Use per-branch structured logger with item_id; flush at branch end.
antipattern.detector: Stress-test 50 items; assert per-item log lines are atomic.
